plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet mandate.
Sell all holdings flagged by the exit scanner (QQQI, QQQ, SMH) and all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to preserve capital.
Hold a significant cash buffer of 20% to manage risk.
Buy XLF and RTX to diversify the portfolio into new, high-scoring sectors.
Maintain single name exposure below 20% and sector exposure below 45% for stability.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5%/month, focusing on capital preservation. Expect lower volatility and drawdowns due to reduced leverage and diversified holdings. Risk is primarily from broad market corrections impacting remaining equity positions.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits.
Sell QQQI, QQQ, and SMH as they are flagged by the exit scanner.
Reduce SOXL to 5% and sell SPAL to limit leveraged ETF exposure.
Hold a 10% cash buffer to maintain flexibility.
Buy XLF and AMD to add high-scoring, diversified growth opportunities.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for ~2.5%/month, prioritizing risk-first growth. It blends broad index exposure with quality tech and limited leverage. Expect moderate volatility and drawdowns, with risk managed by diversification and reduced leverage.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with concentration limits.
Sell QQQI, QQQ, and SMH as they are flagged by the exit scanner.
Sell SPAL as it is below its stop, but increase SOXL to 15% for leveraged growth.
Maintain a small 5% cash buffer to maximize deployed capital.
Buy AMD and META, focusing on high-scoring tech setups to drive aggressive growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month, aiming for stretch growth. Expect significantly higher volatility and potential drawdowns due to increased leverage and concentration. Leveraged ETFs decay daily, magnifying losses if not managed with strict stop discipline.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% and CRWD to 5% to reduce concentration and high-beta exposure.
Sell QQQ, SMH, SOXL, and SPAL to eliminate downtrending and leveraged assets.
Increase QQQI to 25% to prioritize income generation.
Hold a 15% cash buffer for liquidity and stability.
Buy XLF to add diversification and potential yield to the portfolio.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month, aiming to park cash for yield. It prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown over growth. Note that distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in selloffs; this is not a savings account.
